animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season..

animal housing producing Eggs, hen, in shell in Canada. Additional description: In multi-tier free run systems, hens live either directly on the floor or within an aviary structure, with access to perches, nest and scratch areas, and other enrichments.. Additional Context: Based on the provided context, the appropriate allocation method for the animal housing producing Eggs, hen, in shell in Canada is:Economic allocation.This is because the study aims to compare results with previous studies that used economic allocation, and it is also appealing since the economic value of poultry is relatively small compared to the value of eggs. Additionally, the study mentions that economic allocation is the last of the steps described in the ISO 14044 standard and is sometimes interpreted as the least desirable, but it is chosen here due to its practical approach.

animal housing producing Broiler chicken in Netherlands. Additional description: Broiler production - Conventional. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the life cycle assessment (LCA) relevant information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes:	+ Animal housing (broiler chicken production)	+ Feed production	+ Slaughterhouse	+ Packaging* Excluded:	+ Solid-stream processing (outside the slaughterhouse)	+ Production of cleaning agents	+ Composting of manure (outside the system boundaries)	+ Production of vaccines and fungicides	+ Production of one-day-old chicks**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) as suggested by LEAP (2014)**Control Volumes:*** Distinct control volumes for:	+ Grower (who owns and operates the houses)	+ Integrator (who owns the birds and feed)* Combined control volume for the entire broiler production system**Life Cycle Inventory:*** Includes direct and indirect inputs and emissions from:	+ Feed production	+ Energy sources	+ Bedding	+ Packaging materials	+ Water usage and wastewater treatment

animal housing producing Broiler chicken, hen in Thailand. Additional description: CB2-Parent farm 1. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes:	+ Animal housing (broiler chicken production)	+ Feed production	+ Vaccines and cleaning production	+ Slaughterhouse	+ Packaging process* Excluded from the system boundaries:	+ Solid-stream processing (outside the slaughterhouse)	+ Production of cleaning agents (outside the slaughterhouse)	+ Composting of manure (outside the system boundaries)	+ Production of animal drugs and chicks (outside the system boundaries)**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) is used as the functional unit for the LCA assessment.**Life Cycle Stages:*** Upstream: Chick production and feed production* Downstream: Slaughterhouse, packaging, and distribution**Control Volumes:*** Distinct control volumes for the Grower (who owns and operates the houses) and the Integrator (who owns the birds and feed)* Combined control volume that encompasses the overall costs and life cycle impacts for the entire broiler production system.
